The maximum number of outstanding QoS1 and QoS2 messages that the Client has signaled it is willing to accept. Note that there are other broker settings which may further limit the number of outstanding messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.21.

will.description:
Indicates whether the MQTT Session has the Will message specified by the Client. The Will message is published if the Client disconnects without sending the MQTT DISCONNECT packet.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

count.description: The total number of objects in the subscriptions collection.

mqttConnackErrorTxCount.description:
The number of MQTT connect acknowledgment (CONNACK) refused response packets transmitted to the Client.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttConnackTxCount.description:
The number of MQTT connect acknowledgment (CONNACK) accepted response packets transmitted to the Client.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttConnectRxCount.description:
The number of MQTT connect (CONNECT) request packets received from the Client.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttDisconnectRxCount.description:
The number of MQTT disconnect (DISCONNECT) request packets received from the Client.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPubcompTxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish complete (PUBCOMP) packets transmitted to the Client in response to a PUBREL packet. These packets are the fourth and final packet of a QoS 2 protocol exchange.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPublishQos0RxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish message (PUBLISH) request packets received from the Client for QoS 0 message delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPublishQos0TxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish message (PUBLISH) request packets transmitted to the Client for QoS 0 message delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPublishQos1RxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish message (PUBLISH) request packets received from the Client for QoS 1 message delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPublishQos1TxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish message (PUBLISH) request packets transmitted to the Client for QoS 1 message delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPublishQos2RxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish message (PUBLISH) request packets received from the Client for QoS 2 message delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPubrecTxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish received (PUBREC) packets transmitted to the Client in response to a PUBLISH packet with QoS 2. These packets are the second packet of a QoS 2 protocol exchange.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

mqttPubrelRxCount.description:
The number of MQTT publish release (PUBREL) packets received from the Client in response to a PUBREC packet. These packets are the third packet of a QoS 2 protocol exchange.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

MsgVpnMqttSessionCounter.description: The counters for the MQTT Session. Deprecated since 2.13. All attributes in this object have been moved to the MsgVpnMqttSession object.

subscriptionsUri.description: The URI of this MQTT Session's collection of Subscription objects.

uri.description: The URI of this MQTT Session object.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnMqttSessionQueueEventBindCountThre.description: Thresholds for the high number of the MQTT Session Queue Consumers Event, relative to `queueMaxBindCount`. Available since 2.14.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnMqttSessionQueueEventMsgSpoolUsage.description: The threshold for the Message Spool usage event of the MQTT Session Queue, relative to `queueMaxMsgSpoolUsage`. Available since 2.14.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnMqttSessionQueueEventRejectLowPrio.description: The threshold for the maximum allowed number of any priority messages queued in the MQTT Session Queue, relative to `queueRejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`. Available since 2.14.

mqttSessionClientId.description:
The Client ID of the MQTT Session, which corresponds to the ClientId provided in the MQTT CONNECT packet.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

mqttSessionVirtualRouter.description:
The virtual router of the MQTT Session. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"primary" - The MQTT Session belongs to the primary virtual router.
"backup" - The MQTT Session belongs to the backup virtual router.
"auto" - The MQTT Session is automatically assigned a virtual router at creation, depending on the broker's active-standby role.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

subscriptionQos.description:
The quality of service (QoS) for the subscription as either 0 (deliver at most once) or 1 (deliver at least once). QoS 2 is not supported, but QoS 2 messages attracted by QoS 0 or QoS 1 subscriptions are accepted and delivered accordingly.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

subscriptionTopic.description:
The MQTT subscription topic.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Subscription object.

authenticationBasicUsername.description:
The username to use with basic authentication.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

authenticationScheme.description:
The authentication scheme used to connect to the proxy. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"none" - No authentication.
"basic" - Username/password authentication.

enabled.description:
Enable or disable the proxy. When disabled, no connections are initiated to this particular Proxy.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

host.description:
The IP address or host name of the proxy.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

port.description:
The port to connect to on the proxy host.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

proxyName.description:
The name of the proxy.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

proxyType.description:
The type of proxy. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"direct" - Direct connection (no proxy).
"http" - HTTP proxy.

uri.description: The URI of this Proxy object.

accessType.description:
The access type for delivering messages to consumer flows bound to the Queue. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"exclusive" - Exclusive delivery of messages to the first bound consumer flow.
"non-exclusive" - Non-exclusive delivery of messages to bound consumer flows in a round-robin (if partition count is zero) or partitioned (if partition count is non-zero) fashion.

alreadyBoundBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Queue bind failures due to being already bound.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageBindRequestRate.description:
The one minute average of the bind request rate received by the Queue, in binds per second (binds/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.25.

averageRxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate received by the Queue, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageRxMsgRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate received by the Queue, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageTxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate transmitted by the Queue, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageTxMsgRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate transmitted by the Queue, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

bindRequestCount.description:
The number of consumer requests to bind to the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

bindRequestRate.description:
The current bind request rate received by the Queue, in binds per second (binds/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.25.

bindSuccessCount.description:
The number of successful consumer requests to bind to the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

bindTimeForwardingMode.description:
The forwarding mode of the Queue at bind time. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"store-and-forward" - Deliver messages using the guaranteed data path.
"cut-through" - Deliver messages using the direct and guaranteed data paths for lower latency.

clientProfileDeniedDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to being denied by the Client Profile.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

consumerAckPropagationEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the propagation of consumer acknowledgments (ACKs) received on the active replication Message VPN to the standby replication Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

createdByManagement.description:
Indicates whether the Queue was created by a management API (CLI or SEMP).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deadMsgQueue.description:
The name of the Dead Message Queue (DMQ) used by the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

deletedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages deleted from the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deliveryCountEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the ability for client applications to query the message delivery count of messages received from the Queue. This is a controlled availability feature. Please contact support to find out if this feature is supported for your use case.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.19.

deliveryDelay.description:
The delay, in seconds, to apply to messages arriving on the Queue before the messages are eligible for delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.22.

destinationGroupErrorDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to a destination group error.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

disabledBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Queue bind failures due to being disabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

disabledDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to it being disabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

durable.description:
Indicates whether the Queue is durable and not temporary.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

egressEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the transmission of messages from the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

highestAckedMsgId.description:
The highest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Queue that were acknowledged.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

highestMsgId.description:
The highest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

inProgressAckMsgCount.description:
The number of acknowledgment messages received by the Queue that are in the process of updating and deleting associated guaranteed messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

ingressEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the reception of messages to the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

invalidSelectorBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Queue bind failures due to an invalid selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastReplayCompleteTime.description:
The timestamp of the last completed replay for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastReplayFailureReason.description:
The reason for the last replay failure for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastReplayFailureTime.description:
The timestamp of the last replay failure for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastReplayStartTime.description:
The timestamp of the last replay started for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastReplayedMsgTxTime.description:
The timestamp of the last replayed message transmitted by the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastSpooledMsgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the last guaranteed message spooled in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowPriorityMsgCongestionDiscardedMsgCoun.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to low priority message congestion control.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowPriorityMsgCongestionState.description:
The state of the low priority message congestion in the Queue. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"disabled" - Messages are not being checked for priority.
"not-congested" - Low priority messages are being stored and delivered.
"congested" - Low priority messages are being discarded.

lowestAckedMsgId.description:
The lowest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Queue that were acknowledged.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowestMsgId.description:
The lowest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxBindCount.description:
The maximum number of consumer flows that can bind to the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxBindCountExceededBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Queue bind failures due to the maximum bind count being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxDeliveredUnackedMsgsPerFlow.description:
The maximum number of messages delivered but not acknowledged per flow for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSize.description:
The maximum message size allowed in the Queue, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSizeExceededDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum message size being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxMsgSpoolUsage.description:
The maximum message spool usage allowed by the Queue, in megabytes (MB). A value of 0 only allows spooling of the last message received and disables quota checking.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSpoolUsageExceededDiscardedMsgCoun.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum message spool usage being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxRedeliveryCount.description:
The maximum number of times the Queue will attempt redelivery of a message prior to it being discarded or moved to the DMQ. A value of 0 means to retry forever.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxRedeliveryExceededDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum redelivery attempts being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxRedeliveryExceededToDmqFailedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum redelivery attempts being exceeded and failing to move to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxRedeliveryExceededToDmqMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages moved to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ) by the Queue due to the maximum redelivery attempts being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtl.description:
The maximum time in seconds a message can stay in the Queue when `respectTtlEnabled` is `"true"`. A message expires when the lesser of the sender assigned time-to-live (TTL) in the message and the `maxTtl` configured for the Queue, is exceeded. A value of 0 disables expiry.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxTtlExceededDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) in hops being exceeded. The TTL hop count is incremented when the message crosses a bridge.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtlExpiredDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) timestamp expiring.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtlExpiredToDmqFailedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) timestamp expiring and failing to move to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtlExpiredToDmqMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages moved to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ) by the Queue due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) timestamp expiring.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgSpoolPeakUsage.description:
The message spool peak usage by the Queue, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgSpoolUsage.description:
The message spool usage by the Queue, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

networkTopic.description:
The name of the network topic for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

noLocalDeliveryDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to no local delivery being requested.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

otherBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Queue bind failures due to other reasons.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

owner.description:
The Client Username that owns the Queue and has permission equivalent to `"delete"`.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

partitionClientName.description:
The name of the client assigned to the partition if the client is currently bound to the queue. Only relevant for queues implementing an individual partition.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

partitionCount.description:
The count of partitions of the queue. Only relevant for queues with an access type of non-exclusive. When zero, bound clients receive messages round-robin. Otherwise, bound clients receive messages from individually assigned partitions.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.35.

partitionNumber.description:
The partition number. Only relevant for queues implementing an individual partition.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

partitionOperationalCount.description:
The operational count of partitions of the queue. Only relevant for queues with an access type of non-exclusive. This may not match the configured count while scaling is underway.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

partitionQueueName.description:
The name of our partitioned queue. Only relevant for queues implementing an individual partition.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

partitionRebalanceDelay.description:
The delay (in seconds) before a partition rebalance is started once needed.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.35.

partitionRebalanceMaxHandoffTime.description:
The maximum time (in seconds) to wait before handing off a partition while rebalancing.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.35.

partitionRebalanceStatus.description:
The rebalance status of the partitioned queue. Only relevant for queues with an access type of non-exclusive and at least 1 partition. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"ready" - Rebalancing is complete.
"holddown" - Rebalancing will start after delay.
"rebalancing" - Rebalancing is underway.
Available since 2.35.

partitionScaleStatus.description:
The scale status of the partitioned queue. Only relevant for queues with an access type of non-exclusive and at least 1 partition. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"invalid-exclusive" - Exclusive queues have no partitions.
"ready" - Partition scaling is complete.
"scaling-up" - Partitions are being added.
"scaling-down" - Partitions are being removed.
"max-partitioned-queues-exceeded" - Maximum number of partitioned queues has been exceeded.
"max-partitions-exceeded" - Maximum number of partitions has been exceeded for this partitioned queue.
Available since 2.35.

partitionStatus.description:
The status of the partition of the partitioned queue. Only relevant for queues implementing an individual partition. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"unassigned" - Partition is not assigned to a client.
"ready" - Partition is assigned to a client.
"paused" - Partition is being handed off to another client.
"unbound" - Assigned client is not bound.
Available since 2.35.

permission.description:
The permission level for all consumers of the Queue, excluding the owner. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"no-access" - Disallows all access.
"read-only" - Read-only access to the messages.
"consume" - Consume (read and remove) messages.
"modify-topic" - Consume messages or modify the topic/selector.
"delete" - Consume messages, modify the topic/selector or delete the Client created endpoint altogether.

queueName.description:
The name of the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveredMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages transmitted by the Queue for redelivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveryDelayEnabled.description:
Enable or disable a message redelivery delay. When false, messages are redelivered as soon as possible.  When true, messages are redelivered according to the initial, max and multiplier.  This should only be enabled when redelivery is enabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayInitialInterval.description:
The delay to be used between the first 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMaxInterval.description:
The maximum delay to be used between any 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.  Due to technical limitations, some redelivery attempt delays may slightly exceed this value.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMultiplier.description:
The amount each delay interval is multiplied by after each failed delivery attempt.  This number is in a fixed-point decimal format in which you must divide by 100 to get the floating point value. For example, a value of 125 would cause the delay to be multiplied by 1.25.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryEnabled.description:
Enable or disable message redelivery. When enabled, the number of redelivery attempts is controlled by maxRedeliveryCount. When disabled, the message will never be delivered from the queue more than once.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.18.

rejectLowPriorityMsgEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the checking of low priority messages against the `rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`. This may only be enabled if `rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior` does not have a value of `"never"`.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit.description:
The number of messages of any priority in the Queue above which low priority messages are not admitted but higher priority messages are allowed.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior.description:
Determines when to return negative acknowledgments (NACKs) to sending clients on message discards. Note that NACKs cause the message to not be delivered to any destination and Transacted Session commits to fail. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"never" - Silently discard messages.
"when-queue-enabled" - NACK each message discard back to the client, except messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.
"always" - NACK each message discard back to the client, including messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.

replayFailureCount.description:
The number of replays that failed for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayStartCount.description:
The number of replays started for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayState.description:
The state of replay for the Queue. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"initializing" - All messages are being deleted from the endpoint before replay starts.
"active" - Subscription matching logged messages are being replayed to the endpoint.
"pending-complete" - Replay is complete, but final accounting is in progress.
"complete" - Replay and all related activities are complete.
"failed" - Replay has failed and is waiting for an unbind response.

replaySuccessCount.description:
The number of replays that succeeded for the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayedAckedMsgCount.description:
The number of replayed messages transmitted by the Queue and acked by all consumers.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayedTxMsgCount.description:
The number of replayed messages transmitted by the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationActiveAckPropTxMsgCount.description:
The number of acknowledgment messages propagated by the Queue to the replication standby remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationStandbyAckPropRxMsgCount.description:
The number of propagated acknowledgment messages received by the Queue from the replication active remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationStandbyAckedByAckPropMsgCount.description:
The number of messages acknowledged in the Queue by acknowledgment propagation from the replication active remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationStandbyRxMsgCount.description:
The number of messages received by the Queue from the replication active remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

respectDmqEligibleEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of DMQ Eligible for messages in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.49.

respectMsgPriorityEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of message priority. When enabled, messages contained in the Queue are delivered in priority order, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest). Regardless of this setting, message priority is not respected when browsing the queue, when the queue is used by a bridge, or if the queue is partitioned.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

respectTtlEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of the time-to-live (TTL) for messages in the Queue. When enabled, expired messages are discarded or moved to the DMQ.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rxByteRate.description:
The current message rate received by the Queue, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

rxMsgRate.description:
The current message rate received by the Queue, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledByteCount.description:
The amount of guaranteed messages that were spooled in the Queue, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that were spooled in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

transportRetransmitMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that were retransmitted by the Queue at the transport layer as part of a single delivery attempt.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.18.

txByteRate.description:
The current message rate transmitted by the Queue, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

txMsgRate.description:
The current message rate transmitted by the Queue, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

txSelector.description:
Indicates whether the Queue has consumers with selectors to filter transmitted messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

txUnackedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages in the Queue that have been transmitted but not acknowledged by all consumers.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

virtualRouter.description:
The virtual router of the Queue. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"primary" - The endpoint belongs to the primary virtual router.
"backup" - The endpoint belongs to the backup virtual router.
Deprecated since 2.31. This attribute has been deprecated. When Guaranteed Messaging is active, this value is always the virtual router for which Guaranteed Messaging is enabled. Otherwise, this value should be ignored.

xaTransactionNotSupportedDiscardedMsgCou.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Queue due to XA Transactions not being supported.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

count.description: The total number of objects in the msgs collection.
The total number of objects in the priorities collection.
The total number of objects in the subscriptions collection.
The total number of objects in the txFlows collection.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnQueueEventBindCountThreshold.description: The thresholds for the Queue consumer flows event, relative to `maxBindCount`.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnQueueEventMsgSpoolUsageThreshold.description: The thresholds for the message spool usage event of the Queue, relative to `maxMsgSpoolUsage`.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnQueueEventRejectLowPriorityMsgLimi.description: The thresholds for the maximum allowed number of any priority messages queued in the Queue event, relative to `rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`.

msgsUri.description: The URI of this Queue's collection of Queue Message objects.

prioritiesUri.description: The URI of this Queue's collection of Queue Priority objects.

subscriptionsUri.description: The URI of this Queue's collection of Queue Subscription objects.

txFlowsUri.description: The URI of this Queue's collection of Queue Transmit Flow objects.

uri.description: The URI of this Queue object.

attachmentSize.description:
The size of the Message attachment, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

contentSize.description:
The size of the Message content, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deliveryEligibleTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Message is eligible for delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time). Available since 2.22.

dmqEligible.description:
Indicates whether the Message is eligible for the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

dmqEligibleAsPublished.description:
Indicates whether the Message was published as eligible for the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.49.

expiryTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Message expires.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

msgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the Message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

partitionKey.description:
The partition key of the Message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

partitionKeyHash.description:
The partition key hash of the Message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.35.

priority.description:
The priority level of the Message, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

publisherId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the Message publisher.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

queueName.description:
The name of the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveryCount.description:
The number of times the Message has been redelivered.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicatedMateMsgId.description:
The Message identifier (ID) on the replication mate. Applicable only to replicated messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationGroupMsgId.description:
An ID that uniquely identifies this Message within this replication group.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.21.

replicationState.description:
The replication state of the Message. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"replicated" - The Message is replicated to the remote Message VPN.
"not-replicated" - The Message is not being replicated to the remote Message VPN.
"pending-replication" - The Message is queued for replication to the remote Message VPN.

spooledTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Message was spooled in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

ttl.description:
The time-to-live (TTL) in milliseconds of the published message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.49.

undelivered.description:
Indicates whether delivery of the Message has never been attempted.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Queue Message object.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

priority.description:
The level of the Priority, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

queueName.description:
The name of the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledByteCount.description:
The amount of guaranteed messages at this Priority spooled by the Queue, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages at this Priority spooled by the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Queue Priority object.

createdByManagement.description:
Indicates whether the Subscription topic was created by a management API (CLI or SEMP).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

queueName.description:
The name of the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

subscriptionTopic.description:
The topic of the Subscription.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Queue Subscription object.

accessType.description:
The access type for delivering messages to consumer flows. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"exclusive" - Exclusive delivery of messages to the first bound consumer flow.
"non-exclusive" - Non-exclusive delivery of messages to bound consumer flows in a round-robin (if partition count is zero) or partitioned (if partition count is non-zero) fashion.

consumerAckPropagationEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the propagation of consumer acknowledgments (ACKs) received on the active replication Message VPN to the standby replication Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

deadMsgQueue.description:
The name of the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

deliveryDelay.description:
The delay, in seconds, to apply to messages arriving on the Queue before the messages are eligible for delivery. This attribute does not apply to MQTT queues created from this template, but it may apply in future releases. Therefore, to maintain forward compatibility, do not set this value on templates that might be used for MQTT queues.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.22.

durabilityOverride.description:
Controls the durability of queues created from this template. If non-durable, the created queue will be non-durable, regardless of the specified durability. If none, the created queue will have the requested durability. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"none" - The durability of the endpoint will be as requested on create.
"non-durable" - The durability of the created queue will be non-durable, regardless of what was requested.

maxBindCount.description:
The maximum number of consumer flows that can bind.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxDeliveredUnackedMsgsPerFlow.description:
The maximum number of messages delivered but not acknowledged per flow.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSize.description:
The maximum message size allowed, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSpoolUsage.description:
The maximum message spool usage allowed, in megabytes (MB). A value of 0 only allows spooling of the last message received and disables quota checking.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxRedeliveryCount.description:
The maximum number of message redelivery attempts that will occur prior to the message being discarded or moved to the DMQ. A value of 0 means to retry forever.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxTtl.description:
The maximum time in seconds a message can stay in a Queue when `respectTtlEnabled` is `"true"`. A message expires when the lesser of the sender assigned time-to-live (TTL) in the message and the `maxTtl` configured for the Queue, is exceeded. A value of 0 disables expiry.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

permission.description:
The permission level for all consumers, excluding the owner. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"no-access" - Disallows all access.
"read-only" - Read-only access to the messages.
"consume" - Consume (read and remove) messages.
"modify-topic" - Consume messages or modify the topic/selector.
"delete" - Consume messages, modify the topic/selector or delete the Client created endpoint altogether.

queueNameFilter.description:
A pattern used to determine which Queues use settings from this Template. Two different wildcards can be used in the pattern: * and >. Similar to topic filters or subscription patterns, a > matches anything (but only when used at the end), and a * matches zero or more characters but never a slash (/). A > is only a wildcard when used at the end, after a /. A * is only allowed at the end, after a slash (/).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

queueTemplateName.description:
The name of the Queue Template.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveryDelayEnabled.description:
Enable or disable a message redelivery delay. When false, messages are redelivered as soon as possible.  When true, messages are redelivered according to the initial, max and multiplier.  This should only be enabled when redelivery is enabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayInitialInterval.description:
The delay to be used between the first 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMaxInterval.description:
The maximum delay to be used between any 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.  Due to technical limitations, some redelivery attempt delays may slightly exceed this value.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMultiplier.description:
The amount each delay interval is multiplied by after each failed delivery attempt.  This number is in a fixed-point decimal format in which you must divide by 100 to get the floating point value. For example, a value of 125 would cause the delay to be multiplied by 1.25.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryEnabled.description:
Enable or disable message redelivery. When enabled, the number of redelivery attempts is controlled by maxRedeliveryCount. When disabled, the message will never be delivered from the queue more than once.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.18.

rejectLowPriorityMsgEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the checking of low priority messages against the `rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`. This may only be enabled if `rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior` does not have a value of `"never"`.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit.description:
The number of messages of any priority above which low priority messages are not admitted but higher priority messages are allowed.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior.description:
Determines when to return negative acknowledgments (NACKs) to sending clients on message discards. Note that NACKs prevent the message from being delivered to any destination and Transacted Session commits to fail. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"never" - Silently discard messages.
"when-queue-enabled" - NACK each message discard back to the client, except messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.
"always" - NACK each message discard back to the client, including messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.

respectDmqEligibleEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of DMQ Eligible for messages in the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.49.

respectMsgPriorityEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of message priority. When enabled, messages are delivered in priority order, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

respectTtlEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of the time-to-live (TTL) for messages. When enabled, expired messages are discarded or moved to the DMQ.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnQueueTemplateEventBindCountThresho.description: The thresholds for the Queue consumer flows event, relative to `maxBindCount`.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnQueueTemplateEventMsgSpoolUsageThr.description: The thresholds for the message spool usage event of the Queue, relative to `maxMsgSpoolUsage`.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnQueueTemplateEventRejectLowPriorit.description: The thresholds for the maximum allowed number of any priority messages queued in the Queue event, relative to `rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`.

uri.description: The URI of this Queue Template object.

ackedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages delivered and acknowledged by the consumer.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

activationTime.description:
The timestamp of when the bound Flow became active.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

activityState.description:
The activity state of the Flow. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"active-browser" - The Flow is active as a browser.
"active-consumer" - The Flow is active as a consumer.
"inactive" - The Flow is inactive.

activityUpdateState.description:
The state of updating the consumer with the Flow activity. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"in-progress" - The Flow is in the process of updating the client with its activity state.
"synchronized" - The Flow has updated the client with its activity state.
"not-requested" - The Flow has not been requested by the client to provide activity updates.

bindTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Flow bound to the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

clientName.description:
The name of the Client.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

consumerRedeliveryRequestAllowed.description:
Indicates whether redelivery requests can be received as negative acknowledgments (NACKs) from the consumer. Applicable only to REST consumers.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

cutThroughAckedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that used cut-through delivery and are acknowledged by the consumer.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deliveryState.description:
The delivery state of the Flow. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"closed" - The Flow is unbound.
"opened" - The Flow is bound but inactive.
"unbinding" - The Flow received an unbind request.
"handshaking" - The Flow is handshaking to become active.
"deliver-cut-through" - The Flow is streaming messages using direct+guaranteed delivery.
"deliver-from-input-stream" - The Flow is streaming messages using guaranteed delivery.
"deliver-from-memory" - The Flow throttled causing message delivery from memory (RAM).
"deliver-from-spool" - The Flow stalled causing message delivery from spool (ADB or disk).

flowId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the Flow.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

highestAckPendingMsgId.description:
The highest identifier (ID) of message transmitted and waiting for acknowledgment.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastAckedMsgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the last message transmitted and acknowledged by the consumer.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastSelectorExaminedMsgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the last message examined by the Flow selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowestAckPendingMsgId.description:
The lowest identifier (ID) of message transmitted and waiting for acknowledgment.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxUnackedMsgsExceededMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that exceeded the maximum number of delivered unacknowledged messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

noLocalDelivery.description:
Indicates whether not to deliver messages to a consumer that published them.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

queueName.description:
The name of the Queue.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveredMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that were redelivered.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveryRequestCount.description:
The number of consumer requests via negative acknowledgments (NACKs) to redeliver guaranteed messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selector.description:
The value of the Flow selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selectorExaminedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages examined by the Flow selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selectorMatchedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages for which the Flow selector matched.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selectorNotMatchedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages for which the Flow selector did not match.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

sessionName.description:
The name of the Transacted Session for the Flow.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

storeAndForwardAckedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that used store and forward delivery and are acknowledged by the consumer.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

transportRetransmitMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that were retransmitted at the transport layer as part of a single delivery attempt.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.18.

unackedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages delivered but not yet acknowledged by the consumer.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

usedWindowSize.description:
The number of guaranteed messages using the available window size.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

windowClosedCount.description:
The number of times the window for guaranteed messages was filled and closed before an acknowledgment was received.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

windowSize.description:
The number of outstanding guaranteed messages that can be transmitted over the Flow before an acknowledgment is received.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Queue Transmit Flow object.

averageRxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate received by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

averageRxMsgRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate received by the Message VPN, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

averageTxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate transmitted by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

averageTxMsgRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate transmitted by the Message VPN, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

rxByteRate.description:
The current message rate received by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

rxMsgRate.description:
The current message rate received by the Message VPN, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

tlsAverageRxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the TLS message rate received by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

tlsAverageTxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the TLS message rate transmitted by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

tlsRxByteRate.description:
The current TLS message rate received by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

tlsTxByteRate.description:
The current TLS message rate transmitted by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).
