The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

txByteRate.description:
The current message rate transmitted by the Message VPN, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

txMsgRate.description:
The current message rate transmitted by the Message VPN, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.13. This attribute has been moved to the MsgVpn object.

MsgVpnRate.description: The rates for the Message VPN. Deprecated since 2.13. All attributes in this object have been moved to the MsgVpn object.

egressEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the transmission of messages from the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

ingressEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the reception of messages to the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxSpoolUsage.description:
The maximum spool usage allowed by the Replay Log, in megabytes (MB). If this limit is exceeded, old messages will be trimmed.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

msgSpoolUsage.description:
The spool usage of the Replay Log, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayLogName.description:
The name of the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

topicFilterEnabled.description:
Enable or disable topic filtering for the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.27.

count.description: The total number of objects in the msgs collection.
The total number of objects in the topicFilterSubscriptions collection. Available since 2.27.

msgsUri.description: The URI of this Replay Log's collection of Message objects.

topicFilterSubscriptionsUri.description: The URI of this Replay Log's collection of Topic Filter Subscription objects. Available since 2.27.

uri.description: The URI of this Replay Log object.

attachmentSize.description:
The size of the message attachment, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

contentSize.description:
The size of the message content, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

dmqEligible.description:
Indicates whether the message is eligible for the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

priority.description:
The priority level of the message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

publisherId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the message publisher.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayLogName.description:
The name of the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationGroupMsgId.description:
An ID that uniquely identifies this Message within this replication group.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.21.

sequenceNumber.description:
The sequence number assigned to the message. Applicable only to messages received on sequenced topics.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledTime.description:
The timestamp of when the message was spooled in the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

uri.description: The URI of this Message object.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayLogName.description:
The name of the Replay Log.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

topicFilterSubscription.description:
The topic of the Subscription.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Topic Filter Subscription object.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicatedTopic.description:
The topic for applying replication. Published messages matching this topic will be replicated to the standby site.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationMode.description:
The replication mode for the Replicated Topic. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"sync" - Messages are acknowledged when replicated (spooled remotely).
"async" - Messages are acknowledged when pending replication (spooled locally).

uri.description: The URI of this Replicated Topic object.

clientName.description:
The name of the Client for the REST Delivery Point.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

clientProfileName.description:
The Client Profile of the REST Delivery Point. It must exist in the local Message VPN. Its TCP parameters are used for all REST Consumers in this RDP. Its queue properties are used by the RDP client. The Client Profile is used inside the auto-generated Client Username for this RDP.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

enabled.description:
Enable or disable the REST Delivery Point. When disabled, no connections are initiated or messages delivered to any of the contained REST Consumers.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

gatewayReplaceTargetAuthorityEnabled.description:
Enable or disable whether the authority for the request-target is replaced with that configured for the REST Consumer remote. When enabled, the broker sends HTTP requests in absolute-form, with the request-target's authority taken from the REST Consumer's remote host and port configuration. When disabled, the broker sends HTTP requests whose request-target matches that of the original request message, including whether to use absolute-form or origin-form. This configuration is applicable only when the Message VPN is in REST gateway mode.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

lastFailureReason.description:

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastFailureTime.description:

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

postRequestTarget.description:
The request-target string to use when sending requests. It identifies the target resource on the far-end REST Consumer upon which to apply the request. There are generally two common forms for the request-target. The origin-form is most often used in practice and contains the path and query components of the target URI. If the path component is empty then the client must generally send a "/" as the path. When making a request to a proxy, most often the absolute-form is required. This configuration is only applicable when the Message VPN is in REST messaging mode.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

requestTargetEvaluation.description:
The type of evaluation to perform on the request target. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"none" - Do not evaluate substitution expressions on the request target.
"substitution-expressions" - Evaluate substitution expressions on the request target.
Available since 2.23.

authenticationScheme.description:
The authentication scheme used by the REST Consumer to login to the REST host. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"none" - Login with no authentication. This may be useful for anonymous connections or when a REST Consumer does not require authentication.
"http-basic" - Login with a username and optional password according to HTTP Basic authentication as per RFC 2616.
"client-certificate" - Login with a client TLS certificate as per RFC 5246. Client certificate authentication is only available on TLS connections.
"http-header" - Login with a specified HTTP header.
"oauth-client" - Login with OAuth 2.0 client credentials.
"oauth-jwt" - Login with OAuth (RFC 7523 JWT Profile).
"transparent" - Login using the Authorization header from the message properties, if present. Transparent authentication passes along existing Authorization header metadata instead of discarding it. Note that if the message is coming from a REST producer, the REST service must be configured to forward the Authorization header.
"aws" - Login using AWS Signature Version 4 authentication (AWS4-HMAC-SHA256).

enabled.description:
Enable or disable the REST Consumer. When disabled, no connections are initiated or messages delivered to this particular REST Consumer.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

httpMethod.description:
The HTTP method to use (POST or PUT). This is used only when operating in the REST service "messaging" mode and is ignored in "gateway" mode. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"post" - Use the POST HTTP method.
"put" - Use the PUT HTTP method.
Available since 2.17.

tlsCipherSuiteList.description:
The colon-separated list of cipher suites the REST Consumer uses in its encrypted connection when using TLS 1.2 and lower. The value `"default"` implies all supported suites ordered from most secure to least secure. The list of default cipher suites is available in the `tlsCipherSuiteMsgBackboneDefaultList` attribute of the Broker object in the Monitoring API. The REST Consumer should choose the first suite from this list that it supports.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

receiverTcpKeepaliveInterval.description:
The amount of time between TCP keepalive retransmissions to a client using the Client Profile when no acknowledgment is received, in seconds.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

receiverTcpMaxSegmentSize.description:
The TCP maximum segment size for clients using the Client Profile, in bytes. Changes are applied to all existing connections.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

receiverTcpMaxWindowSize.description:
Obsolete.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Deprecated since 2.48. The value is now ignored.

telemetryProfileName.description:
The name of the Telemetry Profile.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

traceEnabled.description:
Enable or disable generation of all trace span data messages. When enabled, the state of configured trace filters control which messages get traced. When disabled, trace span data messages are never generated, regardless of the state of trace filters.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

traceSendSpanGenerationEnabled.description:
Enable or disable generation of send spans. For the most complete view of broker message processing, this should be enabled. If the information provided by send spans are not needed, send spans can be disabled to reduce the performance impact of tracing.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.36.

count.description: The total number of objects in the receiverAclConnectExceptions collection.
The total number of objects in the traceFilters collection.

receiverAclConnectExceptionsUri.description: The URI of this Telemetry Profile's collection of Receiver ACL Connect Exception objects.

traceFiltersUri.description: The URI of this Telemetry Profile's collection of Trace Filter objects.

uri.description: The URI of this Telemetry Profile object.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnTelemetryProfileQueueEventBindCoun.description: The thresholds for the Queue consumer flows event, relative to `queueMaxBindCount`.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnTelemetryProfileQueueEventMsgSpool.description: The thresholds for the message spool usage event of the Queue, relative to `queueMaxMsgSpoolUsage`.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

receiverAclConnectExceptionAddress.description:
The IP address/netmask of the receiver connect exception in CIDR form.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

telemetryProfileName.description:
The name of the Telemetry Profile.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Receiver ACL Connect Exception object.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

MsgVpnTelemetryProfileReceiverEventConne.description: The thresholds for the receiver connection count event, relative to `receiverMaxConnectionCountPerClientUsername`.

enabled.description:
Enable or disable the trace filter. When the filter is disabled, the filter's subscriptions will not trigger a message to be traced.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

telemetryProfileName.description:
The name of the Telemetry Profile.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

traceFilterName.description:
A name used to identify the trace filter. Consider a name that describes the subscriptions contained within the filter, such as the name of the application and/or the scenario in which the trace filter might be enabled, such as "appNameDebug".

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

count.description: The total number of objects in the subscriptions collection.

subscriptionsUri.description: The URI of this Trace Filter's collection of Telemetry Trace Filter Subscription objects.

uri.description: The URI of this Trace Filter object.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

subscription.description:
Messages matching this subscription will follow this filter's configuration.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

subscriptionSyntax.description:
The syntax of the trace filter subscription. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"smf" - Subscription uses SMF syntax.
"mqtt" - Subscription uses MQTT syntax.

telemetryProfileName.description:
The name of the Telemetry Profile.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

traceFilterName.description:
A name used to identify the trace filter. Consider a name that describes the subscriptions contained within the filter, such as the name of the application and/or the scenario in which the trace filter might be enabled, such as "appNameDebug".

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Telemetry Trace Filter Subscription object.

accessType.description:
The access type for delivering messages to consumer flows bound to the Topic Endpoint. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"exclusive" - Exclusive delivery of messages to the first bound consumer flow.
"non-exclusive" - Non-exclusive delivery of messages to bound consumer flows in a round-robin fashion.

alreadyBoundBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Topic Endpoint bind failures due to being already bound.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageBindRequestRate.description:
The one minute average of the bind request rate received by the Topic Endpoint, in binds per second (binds/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.25.

averageRxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate received by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageRxMsgRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate received by the Topic Endpoint, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageTxByteRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate transmitted by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

averageTxMsgRate.description:
The one minute average of the message rate transmitted by the Topic Endpoint, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

bindRequestCount.description:
The number of consumer requests to bind to the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

bindRequestRate.description:
The current bind request rate received by the Topic Endpoint, in binds per second (binds/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.25.

bindSuccessCount.description:
The number of successful consumer requests to bind to the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

bindTimeForwardingMode.description:
The forwarding mode of the Topic Endpoint at bind time. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"store-and-forward" - Deliver messages using the guaranteed data path.
"cut-through" - Deliver messages using the direct and guaranteed data paths for lower latency.

clientProfileDeniedDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to being denied by the Client Profile.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

consumerAckPropagationEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the propagation of consumer acknowledgments (ACKs) received on the active replication Message VPN to the standby replication Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

createdByManagement.description:
Indicates whether the Topic Endpoint was created by a management API (CLI or SEMP).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deadMsgQueue.description:
The name of the Dead Message Queue (DMQ) used by the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

deletedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages deleted from the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deliveryCountEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the ability for client applications to query the message delivery count of messages received from the Topic Endpoint. This is a controlled availability feature. Please contact support to find out if this feature is supported for your use case.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.19.

deliveryDelay.description:
The delay, in seconds, to apply to messages arriving on the Topic Endpoint before the messages are eligible for delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.22.

destinationGroupErrorDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to a destination group error.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

destinationTopic.description:
The destination topic of the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

disabledBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Topic Endpoint bind failures due to being disabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

disabledDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to it being disabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

durable.description:
Indicates whether the Topic Endpoint is durable and not temporary.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

egressEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the transmission of messages from the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

highestAckedMsgId.description:
The highest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Topic Endpoint that were acknowledged.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

highestMsgId.description:
The highest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

inProgressAckMsgCount.description:
The number of acknowledgment messages received by the Topic Endpoint that are in the process of updating and deleting associated guaranteed messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

ingressEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the reception of messages to the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

invalidSelectorBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Topic Endpoint bind failures due to an invalid selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastReplayCompleteTime.description:
The timestamp of the last completed replay for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastReplayFailureReason.description:
The reason for the last replay failure for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastReplayFailureTime.description:
The timestamp of the last replay failure for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastReplayStartTime.description:
The timestamp of the last replay started for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastReplayedMsgTxTime.description:
The timestamp of the last replayed message transmitted by the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

lastSelectorExaminedMsgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the last message examined by the Topic Endpoint selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lastSpooledMsgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the last guaranteed message spooled in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowPriorityMsgCongestionDiscardedMsgCoun.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to low priority message congestion control.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowPriorityMsgCongestionState.description:
The state of the low priority message congestion in the Topic Endpoint. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"disabled" - Messages are not being checked for priority.
"not-congested" - Low priority messages are being stored and delivered.
"congested" - Low priority messages are being discarded.

lowestAckedMsgId.description:
The lowest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Topic Endpoint that were acknowledged.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

lowestMsgId.description:
The lowest identifier (ID) of guaranteed messages in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxBindCount.description:
The maximum number of consumer flows that can bind to the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxBindCountExceededBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Topic Endpoint bind failures due to the maximum bind count being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxDeliveredUnackedMsgsPerFlow.description:
The maximum number of messages delivered but not acknowledged per flow for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxEffectiveBindCount.description:
The effective maximum number of consumer flows that can bind to the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Deprecated since 2.47. This attribute has been deprecated. The effective maximum bind count now always matches the configured bind count. Use maxBindCount as a replacement.

maxMsgSize.description:
The maximum message size allowed in the Topic Endpoint, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSizeExceededDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum message size being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxMsgSpoolUsageExceededDiscardedMsgCoun.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum message spool usage being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxRedeliveryCount.description:
The maximum number of times the Topic Endpoint will attempt redelivery of a message prior to it being discarded or moved to the DMQ. A value of 0 means to retry forever.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxRedeliveryExceededDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum redelivery attempts being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxRedeliveryExceededToDmqFailedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum redelivery attempts being exceeded and failing to move to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxRedeliveryExceededToDmqMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages moved to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ) by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum redelivery attempts being exceeded.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxSpoolUsage.description:
The maximum message spool usage allowed by the Topic Endpoint, in megabytes (MB). A value of 0 only allows spooling of the last message received and disables quota checking.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxTtl.description:
The maximum time in seconds a message can stay in the Topic Endpoint when `respectTtlEnabled` is `"true"`. A message expires when the lesser of the sender assigned time-to-live (TTL) in the message and the `maxTtl` configured for the Topic Endpoint, is exceeded. A value of 0 disables expiry.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxTtlExceededDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) in hops being exceeded. The TTL hop count is incremented when the message crosses a bridge.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtlExpiredDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) timestamp expiring.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtlExpiredToDmqFailedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) timestamp expiring and failing to move to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

maxTtlExpiredToDmqMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages moved to the Dead Message Queue (DMQ) by the Topic Endpoint due to the maximum time-to-live (TTL) timestamp expiring.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgSpoolPeakUsage.description:
The message spool peak usage by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgSpoolUsage.description:
The message spool usage by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

networkTopic.description:
The name of the network topic for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

noLocalDeliveryDiscardedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages discarded by the Topic Endpoint due to no local delivery being requested.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

otherBindFailureCount.description:
The number of Topic Endpoint bind failures due to other reasons.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

owner.description:
The Client Username that owns the Topic Endpoint and has permission equivalent to `"delete"`.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

permission.description:
The permission level for all consumers of the Topic Endpoint, excluding the owner. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"no-access" - Disallows all access.
"read-only" - Read-only access to the messages.
"consume" - Consume (read and remove) messages.
"modify-topic" - Consume messages or modify the topic/selector.
"delete" - Consume messages, modify the topic/selector or delete the Client created endpoint altogether.

redeliveredMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages transmitted by the Topic Endpoint for redelivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveryDelayEnabled.description:
Enable or disable a message redelivery delay. When false, messages are redelivered as-soon-as-possible.  When true, messages are redelivered according to the initial, max and multiplier.  This should only be enabled when redelivery is enabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayInitialInterval.description:
The delay to be used between the first 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMaxInterval.description:
The maximum delay to be used between any 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.  Due to technical limitations, some redelivery attempt delays may slightly exceed this value.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMultiplier.description:
The amount each delay interval is multiplied by after each failed delivery attempt.  This number is in a fixed-point decimal format in which you must divide by 100 to get the floating point value. For example, a value of 125 would cause the delay to be multiplied by 1.25.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryEnabled.description:
Enable or disable message redelivery. When enabled, the number of redelivery attempts is controlled by maxRedeliveryCount. When disabled, the message will never be delivered from the topic-endpoint more than once.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.18.

rejectLowPriorityMsgEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the checking of low priority messages against the `rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`. This may only be enabled if `rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior` does not have a value of `"never"`.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit.description:
The number of messages of any priority in the Topic Endpoint above which low priority messages are not admitted but higher priority messages are allowed.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior.description:
Determines when to return negative acknowledgments (NACKs) to sending clients on message discards. Note that NACKs cause the message to not be delivered to any destination and Transacted Session commits to fail. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"never" - Silently discard messages.
"when-topic-endpoint-enabled" - NACK each message discard back to the client, except messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.
"always" - NACK each message discard back to the client, including messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.

replayFailureCount.description:
The number of replays that failed for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayStartCount.description:
The number of replays started for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayState.description:
The state of replay for the Topic Endpoint. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"initializing" - All messages are being deleted from the endpoint before replay starts.
"active" - Subscription matching logged messages are being replayed to the endpoint.
"pending-complete" - Replay is complete, but final accounting is in progress.
"complete" - Replay and all related activities are complete.
"failed" - Replay has failed and is waiting for an unbind response.

replaySuccessCount.description:
The number of replays that succeeded for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayedAckedMsgCount.description:
The number of replayed messages transmitted by the Topic Endpoint and acked by all consumers.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replayedTxMsgCount.description:
The number of replayed messages transmitted by the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationActiveAckPropTxMsgCount.description:
The number of acknowledgment messages propagated by the Topic Endpoint to the replication standby remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationStandbyAckPropRxMsgCount.description:
The number of propagated acknowledgment messages received by the Topic Endpoint from the replication active remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationStandbyAckedByAckPropMsgCount.description:
The number of messages acknowledged in the Topic Endpoint by acknowledgment propagation from the replication active remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationStandbyRxMsgCount.description:
The number of messages received by the Topic Endpoint from the replication active remote Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

respectDmqEligibleEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of DMQ Eligible for messages in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.49.

respectMsgPriorityEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of message priority. When enabled, messages contained in the Topic Endpoint are delivered in priority order, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

respectTtlEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of the time-to-live (TTL) for messages in the Topic Endpoint. When enabled, expired messages are discarded or moved to the DMQ.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rxByteRate.description:
The current message rate received by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

rxMsgRate.description:
The current message rate received by the Topic Endpoint, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

rxSelector.description:
Indicates whether the Topic Endpoint has a selector to filter received messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selector.description:
The value of the receive selector for the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selectorExaminedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages examined by the Topic Endpoint selector.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selectorMatchedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages for which the Topic Endpoint selector matched.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

selectorNotMatchedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages for which the Topic Endpoint selector did not match.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledByteCount.description:
The amount of guaranteed messages that were spooled in the Topic Endpoint, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that were spooled in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

topicEndpointName.description:
The name of the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

transportRetransmitMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages that were retransmitted by the Topic Endpoint at the transport layer as part of a single delivery attempt.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.18.

txByteRate.description:
The current message rate transmitted by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes per second (B/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

txMsgRate.description:
The current message rate transmitted by the Topic Endpoint, in messages per second (msg/sec).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

txUnackedMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages in the Topic Endpoint that have been transmitted but not acknowledged by all consumers.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

virtualRouter.description:
The virtual router used by the Topic Endpoint. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"primary" - The endpoint belongs to the primary virtual router.
"backup" - The endpoint belongs to the backup virtual router.
Deprecated since 2.31. This attribute has been deprecated. When Guaranteed Messaging is active, this value is always the virtual router for which Guaranteed Messaging is enabled. Otherwise, this value should be ignored.

count.description: The total number of objects in the msgs collection.
The total number of objects in the priorities collection.
The total number of objects in the txFlows collection.

clearPercent.description:
The clear threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

clearValue.description:
The clear threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Falling below this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setPercent.description:
The set threshold for the value of this counter as a percentage of its maximum value. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

setValue.description:
The set threshold for the absolute value of this counter. Exceeding this value will trigger a corresponding event.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

msgsUri.description: The URI of this Topic Endpoint's collection of Topic Endpoint Message objects.

prioritiesUri.description: The URI of this Topic Endpoint's collection of Topic Endpoint Priority objects.

txFlowsUri.description: The URI of this Topic Endpoint's collection of Topic Endpoint Transmit Flow objects.

uri.description: The URI of this Topic Endpoint object.

attachmentSize.description:
The size of the Message attachment, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

contentSize.description:
The size of the Message content, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

deliveryEligibleTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Message is eligible for delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time). Available since 2.22.

dmqEligible.description:
Indicates whether the Message is eligible for the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

dmqEligibleAsPublished.description:
Indicates whether the Message was published as eligible for the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.49.

expiryTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Message expires.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

msgId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the Message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

priority.description:
The priority level of the Message, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

publisherId.description:
The identifier (ID) of the Message publisher.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

redeliveryCount.description:
The number of times the Message has been redelivered.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicatedMateMsgId.description:
The Message identifier (ID) on the replication mate. Applicable only to replicated messages.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

replicationGroupMsgId.description:
An ID that uniquely identifies this Message within this replication group.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.21.

replicationState.description:
The replication state of the Message. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"replicated" - The Message is replicated to the remote Message VPN.
"not-replicated" - The Message is not being replicated to the remote Message VPN.
"pending-replication" - The Message is queued for replication to the remote Message VPN.

spooledTime.description:
The timestamp of when the Message was spooled in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". This value represents the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC (Unix time).

topicEndpointName.description:
The name of the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

ttl.description:
The time-to-live (TTL) in milliseconds of the published message.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". Available since 2.49.

undelivered.description:
Indicates whether delivery of the Message has never been attempted.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Topic Endpoint Message object.

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

priority.description:
The level of the Priority, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledByteCount.description:
The amount of guaranteed messages at this Priority spooled by the Topic Endpoint, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

spooledMsgCount.description:
The number of guaranteed messages at this Priority spooled by the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

topicEndpointName.description:
The name of the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

uri.description: The URI of this Topic Endpoint Priority object.

accessType.description:
The access type for delivering messages to consumer flows. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"exclusive" - Exclusive delivery of messages to the first bound consumer flow.
"non-exclusive" - Non-exclusive delivery of messages to bound consumer flows in a round-robin fashion.

consumerAckPropagationEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the propagation of consumer acknowledgments (ACKs) received on the active replication Message VPN to the standby replication Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

deadMsgQueue.description:
The name of the Dead Message Queue (DMQ).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

deliveryDelay.description:
The delay, in seconds, to apply to messages arriving on the Topic Endpoint before the messages are eligible for delivery.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.22.

maxBindCount.description:
The maximum number of consumer flows that can bind.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxDeliveredUnackedMsgsPerFlow.description:
The maximum number of messages delivered but not acknowledged per flow.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSize.description:
The maximum message size allowed, in bytes (B).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxMsgSpoolUsage.description:
The maximum message spool usage allowed, in megabytes (MB). A value of 0 only allows spooling of the last message received and disables quota checking.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxRedeliveryCount.description:
The maximum number of message redelivery attempts that will occur prior to the message being discarded or moved to the DMQ. A value of 0 means to retry forever.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

maxTtl.description:
The maximum time in seconds a message can stay in the Topic Endpoint when `respectTtlEnabled` is `"true"`. A message expires when the lesser of the sender assigned time-to-live (TTL) in the message and the `maxTtl` configured for the Topic Endpoint, is exceeded. A value of 0 disables expiry.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

msgVpnName.description:
The name of the Message VPN.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

permission.description:
The permission level for all consumers, excluding the owner. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"no-access" - Disallows all access.
"read-only" - Read-only access to the messages.
"consume" - Consume (read and remove) messages.
"modify-topic" - Consume messages or modify the topic/selector.
"delete" - Consume messages, modify the topic/selector or delete the Client created endpoint altogether.

redeliveryDelayEnabled.description:
Enable or disable a message redelivery delay. When false, messages are redelivered as-soon-as-possible.  When true, messages are redelivered according to the initial, max and multiplier.  This should only be enabled when redelivery is enabled.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayInitialInterval.description:
The delay to be used between the first 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMaxInterval.description:
The maximum delay to be used between any 2 redelivery attempts.  This value is in milliseconds.  Due to technical limitations, some redelivery attempt delays may slightly exceed this value.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryDelayMultiplier.description:
The amount each delay interval is multiplied by after each failed delivery attempt.  This number is in a fixed-point decimal format in which you must divide by 100 to get the floating point value. For example, a value of 125 would cause the delay to be multiplied by 1.25.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.33.

redeliveryEnabled.description:
Enable or disable message redelivery. When enabled, the number of redelivery attempts is controlled by maxRedeliveryCount. When disabled, the message will never be delivered from the topic-endpoint more than once.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.18.

rejectLowPriorityMsgEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the checking of low priority messages against the `rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit`. This may only be enabled if `rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior` does not have a value of `"never"`.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectLowPriorityMsgLimit.description:
The number of messages that are permitted before low priority messages are rejected.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

rejectMsgToSenderOnDiscardBehavior.description:
Determines when to return negative acknowledgments (NACKs) to sending clients on message discards. Note that NACKs cause the message to not be delivered to any destination and Transacted Session commits to fail. The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.) The allowed values and their meaning are: 
"never" - Silently discard messages.
"when-topic-endpoint-enabled" - NACK each message discard back to the client, except messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.
"always" - NACK each message discard back to the client, including messages that are discarded because an endpoint is administratively disabled.

respectDmqEligibleEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of DMQ Eligible for messages in the Topic Endpoint.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

 Available since 2.49.

respectMsgPriorityEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of message priority. When enabled, messages are delivered in priority order, from 9 (highest) to 0 (lowest).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

respectTtlEnabled.description:
Enable or disable the respecting of the time-to-live (TTL) for messages. When enabled, expired messages are discarded or moved to the DMQ.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

topicEndpointNameFilter.description:
A pattern used to determine which Topic Endpoints use settings from this Template. Two different wildcards can be used in the pattern: * and >. Similar to topic filters or subscription patterns, a > matches anything (but only when used at the end), and a * matches zero or more characters but never a slash (/). A > is only a wildcard when  used at the end, after a /. A * is only allowed at the end, after a slash (/).

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)

topicEndpointTemplateName.description:
The name of the Topic Endpoint Template.

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only".

clearPercent.description:

The minimum access scope/level required to retrieve this attribute is "vpn/read-only". (This description is taken from the corresponding attribute in the configuration API.)
